meta.goal: Build github.com/Autocss-com/autocss = the refined, completed version of `starter`. Measuring stick: the finished app must look, behave, and work like `dhcp` (the working prototype), changing nothing except to improve to the D7460N Architecture. Separate repo so the two originals are never touched.

repos.autocss: this repo - best-of-both: starter's architecture, completed using dhcp as the target, every feature rewritten to D7460N.
repos.starter: the D7460N-correct rewrite of dhcp; CSS-driven visibility done, but incomplete. Architecture reference.
repos.dhcp: messy-but-complete working prototype; the FEATURE + LOOK reference (an MVP, a floor not a ceiling).

rules: CLAUDE.md in this repo is canonical and non-negotiable.
HTML+CSS do everything; JS is CRUD-only (API transport).

JS's single UI bridge is `oninput` on state-machine <input>s - fired by BOTH user action AND programmatic runtime calls (e.g. initial page load). NEVER click/change/addEventListener/dispatchEvent/dynamic import().
No data-*/class/id; drive dirty/valid/enabled via CSS :has(:valid)/:has(:invalid)/:checked/:empty/:not(:empty)/aria-disabled.
JSON -> elements via toTagName() is allowed; no innerHTML string-building for presentation.
No utils/helpers/utility file names - name each module for what it DOES.
Semantic clarity beats cleverness; modern ES6+ baseline; stateless/idempotent; one API base.
Size: smaller is a guideline not a rule; D7460N compliance may need more code - if autocss ends up larger than dhcp, explain why.
meta.heuristic: Per feature (HTML + companion CSS + companion JS together, companion tracking mandatory): (1) what does it do; (2) is there a MORE MODERN native HTML/CSS way - if yes, STOP and ASK the user before changing markup/CSS, every time (identifying != applying); if no, keep unchanged; (3) will the universal 'one UI to rule them all' app ever need it - YES is the overwhelming default = KEEP & MODERNIZE. Dropping is a rare exception: only if truly redundant, and ALWAYS confirm with user first. Partial/half-built features: inspect, default to COMPLETE not drop.
meta.verify: Enabled env: network=Full (data hosts + Playwright CDN), browser=Playwright chromium via setup script. Tests = JS-logic node unit tests + static/compliance checks + Playwright DOM/behavioral/regression. Pixel-perfect bleeding-edge CSS fidelity needs a HUMAN eye (user does this manually).
meta.cadence: File-by-file, feature-by-feature. Stop and ask on ANY doubt. Check in before FIRST push and after each numbered build step. Test each feature + run regression as we go. Keep companions in lockstep.

build_order: 1. Repo skeleton + PROGRESS.json + SessionStart hook + CLAUDE.md.
2. CSS base (starter set; reconcile dhcp concerns; inspect candidate-drops diag/stars/hero/_* and confirm with user before dropping).
3. index.html single universal scaffold (incl. dropdown-capable nav + its CSS).
5. Data shaping: schema(dhcp domain for now)/rules/ + concern-named modules (NEVER utils/helpers) - node tests.
7. CRUD UI forms + forms.css(de-data-*) + fallbacks.css + loading.css.
8. Wire dhcp datasets; full behavioral + regression pass in the enabled env.

_self.on_rotate: start progress/log-NNN.ndjson (next number), register it in shards[], update cursor.
_self.never: never edit past records; only append corrections (record type=lesson or note).
_self.resume: read PROGRESS.json first, then the newest shard, to restore context + why.

cursor.phase: steps 1-5 DONE. Next: step-6 (generation/inject - toTagName, wire oninput seams).
cursor.last: Step 5 data shaping built+tested: schema.js + rules.js (+env.js node-safe) + tests/data-shaping.test.mjs (node --test glob = 7/7). Pushed.
cursor.next: Step 6: inject.js (toTagName JSON->elements, NO innerHTML/events). Implement injectNavItems(navData) [build nav <details><summary><section><label><input type=radio name=nav value=endpoint>] and injectPageContent(endpoint,normalizedRecord) [h1/p + dual-<ul> data table rows + selects via rules]. Wire into oninput.js STEP-6 seams (hydrateShell nav seam + runOnInputLifecycle inject seam), then bindNavOnInput()+triggerInitialSelection() activate the programmatic .click() data lifecycle. Test in chromium over http (ignoreHTTPSErrors). Then step-7 forms, step-8 wire+regression. Also pending: manifest/sw (PWA Q4), color-scheme dark-vs-auto, API-base confirm.
open_q: themes color-scheme default = dark (dhcp fidelity); Rule 19 prefers 'light dark' auto-follow OS. User said proceed; still unconfirmed - flip is 1 line.
Table BODY row cell alignment depends on injected row structure - finalize in steps 5-6.
manifest.webmanifest + sw.js (PWA, ANALYSIS Q4) still pending.
Logo mask-image needs http to render (file:// CORS); fine in prod.
User to VISUALLY verify the two scaffold screenshots vs dhcp.
